story_clusters.summary: External Affairs Minister Jaishankar met with Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov on the sidelines of the BRICS Foreign Ministers' meeting, with trade and geopolitics as key discussion points. Jaishankar also criticized "unilateral" sanctions ahead of the expiration of a U.S. waiver on Russian oil imports. Additionally, he held talks with Chile’s Foreign Minister Mackenna, focusing on trade diversification and multilateral cooperation.

story_clusters.summary: Silver prices in India fell to ₹2.79 lakh per kg, down 4%, amid a stronger dollar and rising crude oil prices, while gold also declined by ₹1,600 due to inflation concerns linked to tensions involving Iran. Investors are closely monitoring the upcoming Trump-Xi meeting, as geopolitical risks, including Iran-related threats to oil supply routes, continue to influence commodity markets. Crude oil prices rose over 1% following warnings about Iran and concerns over the Strait of Hormuz.

story_clusters.summary: During BRICS talks in New Delhi, Iran and the UAE engaged in a dispute while Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov mediated. Meanwhile, tensions over Iran’s nuclear program and potential oil shocks dominated discussions, with Prof Hamed Mousavi advocating for BRICS to play a stronger mediating role. Separately, U.S. President Trump stated that Chinese leader Xi does not want Iran to possess nuclear weapons, and India’s Prime Minister Modi traveled to the UAE.

Officials and reports indicate that current gold imports under the FTA quota remain limited, while some analysts warn of potential increases in imports from Dubai following any duty adjustments.

articles.title: Colony Built, Pipes Not Laid: MP HC Ends SADA-Jal Nigam Buck Passing; Directs Jal Nigam to Ensure Residents’ Right to Potable Water
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court ruled against the buck-passing between the Special Area Development Authority (SADA) and Jal Nigam over unlaid water pipes in a colony. The court directed Jal Nigam to ensure residents receive potable water, emphasizing that government instrumentalities cannot shirk their responsibilities. This decision underscores the right to basic amenities and holds authorities accountable for infrastructure failures.
articles.raw_summary: The Court stated that although it would have been appropriate for the State to proactively coordinate and ensure that its instrumentalities discharge their respective obligations, the failure to do so does not give any right to the Special Area Development Authority (SADA) and Jal Nigam to shed their responsibilities and deny the residents their basic entitlement. articles.summary: The article provides a comprehensive analysis of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (Amendment) Act, 2026. articles.title: Why Should Selection Panel For Election Commissioners Include Cabinet Minister? Third Person Must Be Neutral : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court observed during a hearing that the selection panel for Election Commissioners must include a Cabinet Minister and a third neutral person to ensure the process appears independent. The Court emphasized the need for transparency and impartiality in the selection mechanism. This ruling addresses concerns about the perceived lack of independence in the current system.
articles.raw_summary: The Court observed during the hearing that the selection must also appear to be independent.

articles.title: The toll of structural adjustments on the global South and a case for accountability
articles.summary: In the 1980s, the IMF and World Bank imposed conditions on loans to countries in the global South, including many in Africa and Latin America. Decades later, these countries continue to face weak public health systems and high poverty levels. A new paper argues that these institutions owe reparations for the damage caused by these policies.
articles.raw_summary: In the 1980s, IMF and World Bank put conditions on financing the global South. Decades later, many countries in the regions struggle with weak public health systems and high levels of poverty. The institutions owe reparations for the damage caused, a new paper argues.

articles.title: Bombay HC Quashes GST Registration Cancellation as SCN Was Vague
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court quashed the cancellation of a taxpayer's GST registration because the show cause notice (SCN) lacked clear allegations of fraud, misstatement, or suppression. The court ruled that vague SCNs prevent taxpayers from responding effectively, rendering subsequent orders invalid. This decision underscores the need for precise reasoning in tax enforcement actions.
articles.raw_summary: The Court observed that allegations of fraud, willful misstatement, and suppression must contain clear particulars enabling effective response by the taxpayer. The absence of reasons rendered the show cause notice and subsequent orders invalid. The post Bombay HC Quashes GST Registration Cancellation as SCN Was Vague appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Can IBC Be Invoked to Enforce Purely Contractual Claims? Supreme Court Clarifies
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be invoked to enforce purely contractual claims. The court clarified that insolvency law is not a tool for debt recovery, reinforcing the distinction between contractual disputes and insolvency proceedings. This decision impacts how businesses and creditors approach debt enforcement under the IBC.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court rules that IBC cannot be used to enforce predominantly contractual disputes, reaffirming that insolvency law is not a debt recovery tool.

articles.title: Mere Dispute Over OIDAR Classification Cannot Trigger GST Section 74 Proceedings: Karnataka HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that mere disputes over the classification of services as Online Information Database Access and Retrieval (OIDAR) cannot automatically trigger GST Section 74 proceedings unless the tax department proves deliberate concealment or suppression. The court emphasized that differing interpretations alone do not justify such actions. This ruling impacts how tax authorities assess and enforce GST compliance for OIDAR services.
articles.raw_summary: Court ruled that differing interpretations on classification of services as OIDAR cannot automatically justify invocation of Section 74 unless deliberate concealment or suppression is established by the Department. Senior Advocate K Parmeshwar argued against applying the ERP doctrine, stating it unfairly elevates certain practices. The remarks highlight ongoing debates over religious freedom and constitutional rights in India.

articles.title: Police on alert as racketeers forge papers for organ trade
articles.summary: A statewide raid on May 8 uncovered an illegal organ trade racket where forged documents were used to bypass organ donation laws. Five cases were registered, nine people arrested, and fabricated documents seized. The trade exploits patients' urgent need for transplants and financial incentives for donors. The incident underscores vulnerabilities in current regulations governing organ donation.
articles.raw_summary: A State-wide raid held on May 8 exposed an extensive organ trade racket in which forged documents were used to bypass laws that plug loopholes in organ donation. Five cases were registered, nine people arrested, and fabricated documents seized. The illegal trade thrives on patients’ desperate need for transplant and the monetary benefits that come the donors’ way.

articles.title: Share Premium Addition Removed as ITAT Finds No Cross-Examination of Alleged Entry Operator
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that tax authorities cannot rely on statements from alleged 'entry operators' (tax evasion facilitators) without providing those statements or allowing cross-examination, violating natural justice principles. This decision removes a share premium addition imposed on a taxpayer, setting a precedent for future tax assessments. The ruling underscores the importance of procedural fairness in tax disputes involving alleged accommodation entries.
articles.raw_summary: Tribunal ruled that reliance on statement of an alleged accommodation entry provider without furnishing statement or allowing cross-examination violated principles of natural justice. The post Share Premium Addition Removed as ITAT Finds No Cross-Examination of Alleged Entry Operator appeared first on TaxGuru .

The summit involves central ministries and is expected to influence future policy directions in administrative capacity building.

This ruling highlights the procedural aspects of tax reassessment and the consequences of non-compliance.

articles.title: “Humiliation, degradation, or public shaming of accused violates Art. 21”: Madhya Pradesh HC Directs Inquiry into Alleged Mala-Fide Public Parading of Accused
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court ruled that humiliating or publicly shaming accused individuals violates Article 21 of the Constitution (right to life and personal liberty). The court directed an inquiry into allegations of mala-fide public parading of accused persons. It clarified that mere transportation of accused persons, even on foot, does not automatically violate fundamental rights unless done with deliberate intent to humiliate. The ruling underscores the protection of dignity in legal proc
articles.raw_summary: At the same time, the Court stated that mere transportation of an accused from a police station to the Court for production before the Magistrate, even if done on foot, under unavoidable circumstances, would not ipso facto constitute a violation of fundamental rights, unless it is shown that such an act was done deliberately to humiliate or with mala fide intention. articles.title: EU targets social media giants with new law on ‘addictive design’
articles.summary: The European Union is introducing the Digital Fairness Act to regulate addictive design features on social media platforms like endless scrolling, autoplay, and push notifications. This law builds upon the existing EU Digital Services Act and aims to address concerns about user addiction and digital well-being. The regulation targets major social media companies and could set a precedent for similar policies worldwide, including in India.

The ministry is also preparing a comprehensive employment policy to guide future job creation. These measures seek to improve coordination between labour and industry while addressing workforce challenges.

articles.title: ITAT Condones Appeal Delay as COVID Exemption Period Covered Limitation
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Jodhpur allowed a 30-day delay in filing an income tax appeal, citing the Supreme Court’s COVID-19 limitation exemption period. The case was sent back to the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) for a fresh hearing. The ruling highlights how pandemic-related exemptions can impact legal timelines in tax matters.
articles.raw_summary: The Jodhpur ITAT condoned a 30-day delay in filing an income tax appeal after holding that the delay fell within the Supreme Court’s COVID-19 limitation exemption period. articles.title: GST Insight Portal Mismatch Alone Cannot Justify Section 263 Revision
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Surat ruled that the tax department cannot revise an assessment order under Section 263 of the Income Tax Act solely based on discrepancies in the GST Insight Portal. The tribunal held that the Assessing Officer had already examined the records, and mere portal mismatches were insufficient to deem the original assessment erroneous. This decision underscores the need for substantial evidence beyond data inconsistencies to justify revisions in tax assess
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Surat held that revision under Section 263 was invalid because the Assessing Officer had already conducted inquiries and examined relevant records. Mere discrepancies in Insight Portal GST data were held insufficient to render the assessment order erroneous. articles.title: No Tax on Deemed Rent of Unsold Flats as Builder Treated Them as Business Stock: ITAT Pune
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Pune ruled that unsold flats held as business stock by a real estate developer are not subject to notional rent taxation. The decision was based on prior judicial precedents recognizing such assets as part of business operations. This ruling provides clarity on tax treatment for unsold inventory in the real estate sector.
articles.raw_summary: Tribunal ruled that unsold flats shown as stock-in-trade by a real estate developer cannot attract notional rent taxation. The decision relied on earlier judicial precedents recognizing such assets as part of business operations. The post No Tax on Deemed Rent of Unsold Flats as Builder Treated Them as Business Stock: ITAT Pune appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Restricts Section 14A Disallowance as It Cannot Exceed Exempt Income
articles.summary: The Lucknow Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that disallowances under Section 14A of the Income Tax Act, read with Rule 8D, cannot exceed the exempt dividend income earned by the taxpayer. The tribunal restricted the addition to the actual amount of exempt income, setting a precedent for tax assessments. This decision impacts how tax authorities calculate disallowances for expenses incurred to earn exempt income.
articles.raw_summary: Lucknow ITAT held that disallowance under Section 14A read with Rule 8D cannot exceed the exempt dividend income earned by the assessee. The Tribunal restricted the addition to the actual exempt income amount. The post ITAT Restricts Section 14A Disallowance as It Cannot Exceed Exempt Income appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Titan Company, Kalyan Jewellers, Senco Gold, other stocks fall up to 2% as Centre tightens duty-free gold imports
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) has tightened rules for duty-free gold imports by jewellery exporters, including import caps and mandatory physical inspections. This follows a recent increase in import duties on gold. Companies like Senco Gold, Thangamayil, and Kalyan Jewellers saw their shares dip up to 2% due to these regulatory changes. The move aims to curb gold imports and ensure compliance with trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: New regulations on duty-free gold imports for jewellery exporters have caused shares of Senco Gold, Thangamayil, and Kalyan Jewellers to dip. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade has imposed stricter rules, including import caps and physical inspections, to curb gold imports and ensure compliance. These measures follow a recent increase in import duties.

articles.title: U.P. pushes river rejuvenation initiative to strengthen rural sustainability, ecological resilience
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government revived the Neem River in Bulandshahr through a 29-km intervention under the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA). Farmers voluntarily cleared encroachments to support the project. Officials highlighted the initiative as a step toward strengthening rural sustainability and ecological resilience. The effort reflects state-led environmental governance under existing public welfare schemes.
articles.raw_summary: In Bulandshahr, the Neem River was revived through a 29-km intervention under MGNREGA. Officials said farmers voluntarily cleared encroachments

articles.title: Govt: Right to religion does not provide for gender equality
articles.summary: The Indian government told the Supreme Court that Article 25(1), which guarantees religious freedom, does not require gender equality within religious practices. It argued that Articles 15 and 16 already address sex discrimination and urged courts not to interfere in religious matters, stating that only the legislature can enact social reforms through broader discussions. The Supreme Court has reserved its verdict on the matter.
articles.raw_summary: The Centre argued before the Supreme Court that Article 25(1) on religious freedom doesn't mandate gender equality, as Articles 15 and 16 already prohibit sex discrimination. It urged courts to avoid interfering in religious practices, stating only the legislature can enact social reforms through broad discussions. The bench reserved its verdict.

articles.title: Abandoned Software Development Costs Allowed as Revenue Expense in Adadyn Technologies Case
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that costs incurred on abandoned software development projects can be treated as revenue expenses for tax deduction purposes. The case involved Adadyn Technologies, where the ITAT clarified that no capital asset arises when a software project is permanently abandoned. This ruling provides clarity on tax treatment for businesses facing failed IT projects.

articles.title: Electoral roll revision: EC announces Phase 3 of SIR in 16 states, 3 UTs starting May 30
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India (EC) announced Phase 3 of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in 16 states and 3 Union Territories, starting May 30. articles.title: Buy-back Taxation under Income Tax Act, 2025

The changes also introduce additional tax implications for promoters under Section 69(2). This update impacts how companies and shareholders will be taxed on buy-back transactions, with potential financial consequences for investors and promoters.

articles.title: SC reiterates IBC can’t be used as tool for coercion, debt recovery in breach of contract cases
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India reiterated that the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) cannot be used as a tool for coercion or debt recovery in cases that are primarily contractual disputes. The court upheld a ruling by the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT), emphasizing that insolvency proceedings should not be invoked for breaches of contract. This decision reinforces the original intent of the IBC to address genuine insolvency situations rather than commercial disagreements.
articles.raw_summary: SC said insolvency proceedings can't be invoked for debt recovery in disputes that are predominantly contractual in nature. In doing so, it upheld an NCLAT ruling.

The decision was based on the lack of separate benchmarking by the Transfer Pricing Officer (TPO) and emphasized consistency in pricing approaches. This ruling provides clarity on transfer pricing norms for multinational transactions involving India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that margins agreed under a Bilateral Advance Pricing Agreement may be used for non-covered AEs when transactions are functionally similar. The decision highlights the need for rigorous analysis in transfer pricing disputes under Indian tax law.

articles.title: Less than 1% public vehicles have trackers, panic buttons. SC says no fitness certificate without them
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that public vehicles must have location tracking devices and emergency panic buttons installed to receive a fitness certificate. This requirement was mandated under the Motor Vehicles Act in 2018, following the Nirbhaya case. Currently, less than 1% of public vehicles comply with this rule. The court emphasized that no fitness certificate will be issued without these safety measures.
articles.raw_summary: Location tracking devices and emergency buttons had been made mandatory under Motor Vehicles Act in 2018, two years after Nirbhaya case.

articles.title: Oil prices today: Crude rises over 1% as Trump warns Iran, Hormuz concerns persist
articles.summary: Global oil prices rose over 1% after U.S. President Trump warned about Iran, raising concerns over disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. Brent and WTI crude prices saw weekly gains due to uncertainty over the Iran conflict and supply concerns. The Strait remains a key chokepoint for oil shipments, with maritime incidents adding to market anxieties about tight supply. India, a major oil importer, may face policy implications due to these developments.
articles.raw_summary: Global oil prices surged as President Trump expressed impatience with Iran, fueling fears of Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Brent and WTI crude saw significant weekly gains amid uncertainty over the Iran conflict and supply concerns. Despite claims of increased vessel crossings, the Strait remains a critical choke point, with maritime incidents further heightening market anxieties about tight supply.

articles.title: Benami Property Cannot Be Claimed by Real Owner on the Basis of Benamidar’s Will: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a real owner cannot claim ownership of benami property by relying on a benamidar’s Will. This ruling clarifies legal ambiguities around benami transactions and reinforces the prohibition on using wills to reclaim such properties.

articles.title: Gold duty hike hits demand, raises smuggling concerns across jewellery trade
articles.summary: The Gem and Jewellery Export Promotion Council (GJEPC) warned that a recent increase in gold import duties could push up prices and fuel smuggling in the jewellery trade. articles.title: Sugar export ban to free up 4-5 lakh tonne for domestic use
articles.summary: India has banned sugar exports until September 30. This move aims to increase domestic availability by 4-5 lakh tonnes. The decision is a response to El Nino concerns and potential impacts on future production. While boosting buffer stocks, the ban may affect sugar mill owners' short-term finances. Overseas demand had recently surged, but this ban redirects those supplies inward.

articles.summary: The article explains that courts have consistently ruled that advertisement expenses for brand building are deductible under Section 37 of the Income Tax Act. The ruling impacts how businesses claim tax deductions for advertising expenditures. The analysis is based on judicial interpretations of tax laws.

It argues that their disappearance makes the city harsher and emptier, eroding local identity and memory. The piece is published by Citizen Matters and focuses on the broader implications of environmental degradation in urban spaces.
articles.raw_summary: Bengaluru's trees are more than shade; they are memory, identity, and resistance. Their loss leaves the city harsher and emptier. The post The trees we forget: What a city loses when the canopy disappears appeared first on Citizen Matters .

articles.title: Warning of record global temperatures as chance of very strong El Niño grows
articles.summary: Scientists warn that a developing El Niño event this year could become one of the strongest on record, with global repercussions. The BBC's Simon King explains that the phenomenon may lead to extreme weather patterns worldwide. While the article does not mention India directly, such climate events often require policy responses from governments, including India, to mitigate impacts like heatwaves, droughts, or erratic monsoons.
articles.raw_summary: As El Niño develops this year, scientists are increasingly confident it could be one of the strongest on record with global consequences, as Simon King explains.

articles.summary: Iran has warned that ships entering the Strait of Hormuz must cooperate with its navy after reports emerged of a vessel being seized near the UAE port of Fujairah by unauthorized personnel and taken toward Iranian waters. The UK Maritime Trading Organisation confirmed the ship was docked when seized. The incident highlights regional maritime tensions that could impact global oil supply routes critical to India's energy imports.

articles.title: Xi tells Trump U.S. and China could clash over Taiwan
articles.summary: During a summit in Beijing, U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping exchanged views on sensitive issues, including Taiwan. China claims Taiwan as part of its territory, while the U.S. maintains unofficial ties with the self-ruled island. The discussion highlighted ongoing tensions between the two nations on trade, Iran, and regional security, which could impact global and regional dynamics affecting India.
articles.raw_summary: The exchange at a highly anticipated summit in Beijing underscored just how far apart Trump and Xi still are on thorny issues, including the war in Iran, trade disputes and Washington's relations with Taiwan, which is self-ruled but which China claims as part of its territory.

articles.title: China goes electric, but can it get off coal?
articles.summary: China, the world's largest CO2 emitter, is rapidly expanding its renewable energy capacity while still relying heavily on coal for power. The article explores whether China can reduce its coal dependence as it electrifies its economy with renewables. This shift has global climate implications and may impact India's energy and environmental policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: The world's clean energy superpower also emits the most CO2. But coal-fired power may have peaked as China electrifies its economy with ever more renewables.

articles.title: Hotter 2026 and El Nino could trigger extreme fires
articles.summary: Over 150 million hectares burned globally in early 2026, with forecasts of a supercharged El Nino worsening fires in the latter half of the year. The scale of destruction highlights growing climate risks, which could prompt calls for stronger environmental policies and disaster preparedness measures worldwide, including in India.
articles.raw_summary: More than 150 million hectares — over twice the size of Texas — burned globally in the first months of 2026. With a high chance of a supercharged El Nino, the second half of the year could be even worse.

articles.title: Cabinet raises Minimum Support Prices for 14 kharif crops
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved increased Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for 14 kharif crops for the 2026-27 marketing season. The MSP for all notified kharif crops has been set at least 50% above production costs, with sunflower seed seeing the largest absolute increase. Cotton and other oilseeds also received substantial boosts. The MSP for common paddy has been fixed at Rs 2,441 per quintal.
articles.raw_summary: The Union Cabinet has approved significant hikes in Minimum Support Prices for 14 Kharif crops for the 2026-27 marketing season. Sunflower Seed sees the largest absolute increase. Cotton and other oilseeds also receive substantial boosts. MSPs for all notified Kharif crops are set at least 50% above production costs. Common paddy MSP is fixed at Rs 2,441 per quintal.

Based on current trends, Sen estimates gold imports could decline to nearly 550 tonnes under a conservative scenario.

articles.title: Global refined fuel demand set for steepest decline since ‘Great Recession’: S&P
articles.summary: Global demand for refined fuels like petrol and diesel is expected to decline sharply, the steepest drop since the Great Recession, according to S&P Global. Even if oil supplies resume immediately, delays in recovery are likely due to operational and logistical challenges. This trend could impact India's energy security, fuel pricing, and import strategies, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: According to the report, even if oil flows resume immediately, operational and logistical bottlenecks would delay meaningful recovery in product markets by months rather than weeks.

Consumers are shielded from retail fuel price hikes, but commercial LPG costs increased.

articles.title: Centre raises MSP for 14 kharif crops, boosts support for pulses and oilseeds
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for 14 kharif crops, with the highest hike of 61% for moong. The MSP for paddy (rice) has been raised by Rs 72 per quintal to Rs 2,441 for the common variety. This decision aims to support farmers, particularly for pulses and oilseeds, and is based on expected margins over production costs.
articles.raw_summary: Based on expected margins over cost of production, the MSP hike is the most for moong at 61%, followed by bajra and maize (56% each) and tur (54%). For other crops, it is estimated to be around 50%. The MSP for paddy (rice), the main kharif crop, has been increased by Rs 72 per quintal, taking it to Rs 2,441 for the 'common' variety and Rs 2,461 for 'Grade A'.

articles.title: Retail inflation up a tad on rise in food, bullion prices
articles.summary: Retail inflation reached a four-month peak of 3.5% in April. This rise was fueled by increasing food and gold/silver prices. Economists anticipate further inflationary pressures ahead. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its current policy rate. Inflation is nearing the RBI's target, suggesting a pause in rate hikes for now.
articles.raw_summary: Retail inflation reached a four-month peak of 3.5% in April. This rise was fueled by increasing food and gold/silver prices. Economists anticipate further inflationary pressures ahead. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its current policy rate. Inflation is nearing the RBI's target, suggesting a pause in rate hikes for now.

articles.title: Kharif reset: Farmers may ditch paddy and maize for soybean, pulses amid monsoon fears
articles.summary: As the kharif season approaches, Indian farmers are considering growing soybean and pulses like urad and masur instead of paddy and maize. This shift is driven by concerns over erratic monsoons and potential fertilizer shortages. Maize planting is expected to decline as farmers opt for lower-risk, less fertilizer-dependent crops amid unpredictable weather conditions.
articles.raw_summary: As the kharif season approaches, farmers are leaning towards cultivating oilseeds like soybean and pulses such as urad and masur, primarily due to worries about erratic monsoon patterns and looming fertilizer shortages. Following a recent surge, maize planting is forecasted to decline, as farmers shift towards crops that are less fertilizer-dependent and lower in risk amid unpredictable weather conditions.

articles.title: Global turmoil is India’s 'wake-up call' to cut import dependence: Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urges domestic industry to embrace self-reliance amid global challenges. He calls for increased productivity and better use of trade agreements. The minister emphasizes reducing import dependence to bolster the economy. He also highlights the potential of electric vehicles and advanced technologies like AI for growth. This presents a significant opportunity for India to emerge stronger.
articles.raw_summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urges domestic industry to embrace self-reliance amid global challenges. He calls for increased productivity and better use of trade agreements. The minister emphasizes reducing import dependence to bolster the economy. He also highlights the potential of electric vehicles and advanced technologies like AI for growth. This presents a significant opportunity for India to emerge stronger.

articles.title: D2C brands feel the pinch as consumers cut spending
articles.summary: Rising input costs and shrinking demand are squeezing smaller brands, with consumer spending projected to drop 5-6% next quarter. D2C brands face pressure as inflation and a weakening rupee push consumers towards essential purchases, impacting discretionary spending and average order values. Companies are absorbing costs or planning price hikes up to 10%.
articles.raw_summary: Rising input costs and shrinking demand are squeezing smaller brands, with consumer spending projected to drop 5-6% next quarter. D2C brands face pressure as inflation and a weakening rupee push consumers towards essential purchases, impacting discretionary spending and average order values. Companies are absorbing costs or planning price hikes up to 10%.

articles.title: Dhruva Space gets Rs 105 crore from Centre for developing satellite platform
articles.summary: Dhruva Space secured a Rs 105 crore grant for Project Garud. This initiative focuses on creating a standardized satellite platform. The project aims to build an indigenous manufacturing ecosystem for high-volume satellite deployment. This will support communications, intelligence, and strategic applications. The new platform will enable faster integration and deployment.

articles.title: Soybean and corn futures rise as US says China to buy more American crops

Traders are awaiting further details on the volumes and timing of these purchases. The announcement highlights shifting global agricultural trade dynamics that could influence India's trade policies and market conditions.

articles.title: A rare 'Super' El Niño may be brewing, faster than anyone expected
articles.summary: The U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) reports that a strong or very strong El Niño is forming faster than expected, likely by winter. This could lead to extreme weather events and record global temperatures, which may impact India's monsoon patterns, agriculture, and disaster preparedness.

articles.title: He lived to tell the tale: Man from Bareilly hurled nearly 50 feet in monster storm recounts moment
articles.summary: A severe storm caused widespread destruction across multiple districts in Uttar Pradesh, including Prayagraj, Bhadohi, Fatehpur, Sonbhadra, and Bareilly, resulting in over 100 deaths. The article highlights the personal account of a survivor from Bareilly who was thrown nearly 50 feet during the storm. Such extreme weather events often prompt discussions on disaster management policies and climate adaptation measures by authorities.

articles.title: Air India's $2.8 billion loss drags Singapore Airlines' profit down 57%

Air India posted a loss after tax of nearly $2.8 billion for the financial year ended March 2026- a dramatic widening from just $214 million the previous year.

articles.title: OPEC lowers 2026 oil demand outlook, raises 2027 growth forecast
articles.summary: OPEC has reduced its 2026 oil demand growth forecast to 1.17 million barrels per day (bpd) and lowered its Q2 2026 demand estimate, citing volatile economic conditions. However, it raised the 2027 growth outlook to 1.54 million bpd. The changes reflect shifting global energy trends that may impact India's energy security and import strategies.

articles.title: Fuel price hike inevitable as oil crisis deepens; experts warn worst is yet to come
articles.summary: Industry experts have warned that India’s oil crisis could worsen in the coming months as elevated crude prices, a weak rupee and mounting losses at oil marketing companies increase the likelihood of a retail fuel price hike. Analysts also cautioned that prolonged high energy costs may push inflation higher and weigh on economic growth.

articles.title: India April CPI inflation hits 13-month high at 3.48%, stays below 4% mark
articles.summary: India’s retail inflation rose to a 13-month high of 3.48% in April, driven by higher food and services prices, though the headline print remained below the 4% mark. Food inflation accelerated to 4.20% from 3.87% in March, while core inflation edged up to 3.4%.
articles.raw_summary: India’s retail inflation rose to 3.48% in April from 3.40% in March, driven by higher food and services prices, though the reading remained below CNBC-TV18’s poll estimate of 3.72%. The latest print marks a 13-month high under the revised CPI series, while under the older series it would have been a four-month high.

articles.title: China’s factory inflation surges to a near 4-year high; domestic demand remains tepid
articles.summary: China's factory prices rose by 2.8% in April, the fastest increase since July 2022, ending a period of deflation. The surge is linked to disruptions in energy supplies due to the Iran war, but weak domestic demand and a sluggish labor market are limiting firms' ability to raise prices. The situation highlights broader economic challenges in China that could affect global trade and energy markets.

articles.title: World oil supply to fall below demand in 2026, says IEA, as deadlock in Iran war continues
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) warns that global oil supply could fall below demand by 2026 due to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz caused by the US-Iran conflict. This could drive up Brent Crude prices and affect oil flows through a vital shipping corridor. Iran is also considering new tolls on the Strait, further complicating the situation. The development has implications for energy-importing countries like India, which may face higher fuel costs and supply risks.
articles.raw_summary: IEA warns the US Iran war in the Strait of Hormuz is slashing oil flows, projecting a 2026 supply shortfall and higher Brent Crude, as Iran eyes new tolls on the vital corridor

articles.title: Vijay, MGR, NTR: How film fame became political power in the South
articles.summary: South India has witnessed a unique political culture where cinematic fame often transforms into real political influence. From legends like MGR and N T Rama Rao to contemporary figures like Vijay, film personalities have repeatedly leveraged mass appeal, fan bases, and cultural identity to enter politics. This long-standing phenomenon continues to shape electoral outcomes and redefine leadership in Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and beyond.

Following the court's decision, authorities demolished the corporator's house, which was reportedly sheltering an accused named Nida Khan. The incident highlights issues of illegal construction and property rights enforcement in India. The demolition was carried out a day after the court's ruling.
articles.raw_summary: The demolition process began a day after a local court rejected the AIMIM corporator’s plea seeking more time to respond to notices over alleged illegal construction.

articles.title: Gold, Silver imports to cost more after customs duty, cess hike: Details here
articles.summary: The Indian government has increased the customs duty and cess on gold and silver imports from 6% to 10%, and imposed a 10% duty on platinum and related precious metal products. This decision will raise the cost of importing these metals. The move is part of a fiscal policy adjustment by the government.
articles.raw_summary: With this increase, the total duty on gold and silver now stands at 10% from 6% earlier. Platinum, and related precious metal products, including hooks, clasps, clamps, pins and screw backs used in manufacturing, will also have a 10% duty.

articles.title: For Agra’s footwear artisans, the west Asia war is more than just sand in the shoe
articles.summary: The ongoing war in West Asia is disrupting Agra’s footwear industry, where artisans and exporters face rising costs and delayed supplies. Orders are harder to fulfill on time, squeezing profit margins and threatening recovery even if global tensions ease. The industry warns it may take at least a season to bounce back. Workers and businesses are bearing the brunt of these supply chain disruptions.
articles.raw_summary: Impacts of the West Asia war are now being felt inside Agra’s footwear units, where rising costs and delayed supplies are squeezing workers and exporters alike. With margins thinning and orders getting harder to execute on time, the industry says it may take at least a season to recover even if global tensions ease.

articles.title: HPCL warns of losses in Q1 FY27 as West Asia crisis lifts crude costs, hits fuel margins
articles.summary: State-run refiner HPCL has warned of potential losses in the first quarter of FY27 due to rising crude oil costs driven by the West Asia crisis. While lower-cost crude inventory and strong performance in January-February helped in the previous quarter, the full impact of elevated crude prices is expected to affect earnings in Q1. The company highlighted that the pressure could intensify as higher crude costs flow through its financials.
articles.raw_summary: The state-run refiner said lower-cost crude inventory and strong momentum in January-February helped cushion Q4 performance, but warned that pressure could intensify in Q1 as elevated crude costs fully flow through earnings.

articles.title: Kerala CM-designate VD Satheesan visits VM Sudheeran, ‘seeks blessings’ after Congress nod
articles.summary: Kerala's Congress party has designated VD Satheesan, a six-time MLA from Peravoor, as its new leader. Satheesan, who will turn 62 this month, played a key role in steering the United Democratic Front (UDF) to a landslide victory in the 2026 Assembly Elections. He visited senior Congress leader VM Sudheeran to seek his blessings following the party's nod. This leadership transition may influence the party's future policy priorities and governance approach in Kerala.
articles.raw_summary: The six-time MLA from Peravoor, VD Satheesan, who turns 62 at the end of this month, had steered the UDF's landslide victory in the 2026 Assembly Elections.
